egg: noun. An approximately spherical or ellipsoidal body produced by birds, insects, reptiles, and other animals, often housing an embryo
emigre: noun. One who has departed their native land, often as a refugee.
emir: noun. A prince, commander or other leader or ruler in an Islamic nation.
erg: noun. A unit of work or energy, being the amount of work done by a force of one dyne applied through a distance of one centimeter.

erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
geeing: verb. present participle and gerund of gee
gem: noun. A precious stone, usually of substantial monetary value or prized for its beauty or shine.
gene: noun. A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of
genie: noun. A jinn, a being descended from the jann, normally invisible to the human eye, but who may also appear in animal or human form.
genre: noun. A kind; a stylistic category or sort, especially of literature or other artworks.
germ: noun. The small mass of cells from which a new organism develops; a seed, bud, spore, or zygote.
germen: noun. The basic form of an organism; the seed or germ.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
gig: noun. Originally, a performing engagement by a musician or musical group; a job or role for a performer.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
ginger: noun. The pungent aromatic rhizome of a tropical Asian herb, Zingiber officinale, used as a spice and as a stimulant and acarminative.
gree: noun. One of a flight of steps.

grim: adjective. Dismal and gloomy, cold and forbidding.
grime: noun. Dirt, grease, soot, etc. that is ingrained and difficult to remove.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
mere: adjective. Just, only; no more than, pure and simple, neither more nor better than might be expected.
merge: verb. To combine into a whole.
merging: verb. present participle and gerund of merge
mien: noun. Demeanor; facial expression or attitude, especially one which is intended by its bearer.
mine: pronoun. That or those belonging to me.
miner: noun. A person who works in a mine.
mir: noun. A traditional village community in Imperial Russia, characterised by self-government and collectivist control of local lands.
mire: noun. Deep mud; moist, spongy earth.
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
neem: noun. Azadirachta indica, a large, mostly evergreen tree from India, whose seeds yield neem oil, the insecticide azadirachtin.
nigger: noun. A black person; a person of black African descent.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
regime: noun. Mode of rule or management.
regimen: noun. Orderly government; system of order; administration.
reign: noun. The exercise of sovereign power.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
rem: noun. A non-SI unit of measure for radiation dose, measured as equivalent dose, effective dose, or committed dose, from one roentgen of
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rim: noun. An edge around something, especially when circular.
rime: noun. Archaic in the form rimes: originally, any frozen dew forming a white deposit on exposed surfaces; hoar frost (sense 1).
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
eggier: adjective. comparative form of eggy: more eggy
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
erme: verb. To grieve; to feel sad.
germin: noun. Any of a group of proteins, resistant to proteases, associated with the germination of cereals
germing: verb. present participle and gerund of germ
ggr: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Aghu Tharnggalu.
gim: noun. Edible seaweed in Korean cuisine.
ging: noun. A company; troop; a gang.
inerm: adjective. Without spines or thorns; inermous.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
irene: noun. The hydrocarbon formed by dehydration of an irone.
mei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Midob.
mene: noun. The high middle singing voice (especially as for a boy) or part in musical composition, corresponding roughly to the alto.
mering: verb. present participle and gerund of mere
minge: noun. The pubic hair and vulva.
mri: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Māori.
